---
title: "2.8 for Loops"
subtitle: "Part 2: Decrementing and Mixed Code"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: print_evens`

Textbook prompt: The following method has the correct code to print out all the even values from `0` to the value of `10`, but the code is mixed up.

Drag the blocks into the correct order and indent them correctly. Even though Java doesn't require indention, it is a good habit to get into.

## Mixed Blocks

### `parsonsprob:: print_evens`

```java
public static void printEvens()
{
```

```java
   for (int i = 0; i <= 10; i+=2)
   {
```

```java
      System.out.println(i);
```

```java
   } // end for
```

```java
} // end method
```

## Correct Order

### `parsonsprob:: print_evens`

```java
public static void printEvens()
{
   for (int i = 0; i <= 10; i+=2)
   {
      System.out.println(i);
   } // end for
} // end method
```

## Decrementing Loops

### Count backwards

You can also count backwards in a loop starting from the last number and decrementing down to `0` or `1`.

All 3 parts of the loop must change to count backwards including the test of when to stop.

For example, `for (int i=5; i > 0; i--)` counts from `5` down to `1`.

## Code Task

### `activecode:: for-bottles-backwards`

Textbook prompt: What do you think will happen when you run the code below?

How would it change if you changed line 11 to initialize `i`'s value to `3`?

Try the Code Lens button to visualize and trace through this code.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: for-bottles-backwards`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class SongTest
{

    public static void printPopSong()
    {
        String line1 = " bottles of pop on the wall";
        String line2 = " bottles of pop";
        String line3 = "Take one down and pass it around";

        // loop 5 times (5, 4, 3, 2, 1)
        for (int i = 5; i > 0; i--)
        {
            System.out.println(i + line1);
            System.out.println(i + line2);
            System.out.println(line3);
            System.out.println((i - 1) + line1);
            System.out.println();
        }
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        SongTest.printPopSong();
    }
}
```
:::

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: for-bottles-backwards`

Runestone checks that after the requested edit:

::: {.tight-list}
- the output contains `3 bottles of pop on the wall`
- the first printed song line starts from `3`
- the output does not contain `5 bottles of pop on the wall`
:::

## Trace Note

### `printPopSong`

The method `printPopSong` prints the words to a song.

It initializes the value of the variable `i` equal to `5` and then checks if `i` is greater than `0`.

Since `5` is greater than `0`, the body of the loop executes. Before the condition is checked again, `i` is decreased by `1`.

When the value in `i` is equal to `0`, the loop stops executing.

## Code Task

### `activecode:: forloop-backwards`

Textbook prompt: Can you make the loop count by 2s backwards?

It should print out `5 3 1`.

Remember to change all 3 parts of the for loop.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: forloop-backwards`

```java
public class ForLoop
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        for (int count = 1; count <= 5; count++)
        {
            System.out.println(count);
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: forloop-backwards`

Runestone expects:

```text
5
3
1
```

The loop header must change all 3 parts: initialization, Boolean expression, and update.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- order mixed code blocks into a complete `printEvens` method
- use `i+=2` to visit even values from `0` to `10`
- explain that decrementing loops must change all 3 header parts
- trace why `for (int i=5; i > 0; i--)` stops when `i` becomes `0`
- edit the bottles loop to start at `3`, not `5`
- change a loop so it prints `5`, `3`, and `1`
:::

## End

### Continue to Part 3

[Next: Turtle Shapes Challenge →](2-8-part-3-turtle-shapes-challenge.html)
